methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from a salt marsh and preparing them for foraminiferal analysis.

steps.description: Samples were collected from beneath the Arthrocnemum canopy in two transects beginning at the tidal channel at the seaward side of the low marsh and extending to the maximum extent of halophytic plants in the high marsh.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from marsh transects

steps.description: Samples were collected by inserting the mouth of a 125 ml glass sample jar into the sediment underneath an Arthrocnemum plant to a depth of 1 cm, thereby collecting a plug of surficial sediment 4 cm in diameter and 1 cm thick.
steps.searchable_summary: use glass jar to collect sediment plug

steps.description: Samples were brought back to the lab within a few hours and soaked in a rose Bengal solution (80% ethanol with 5 g rose Bengal powder per litre), for a minimum of two weeks.
steps.searchable_summary: soak samples in rose Bengal solution

steps.description: Samples were gently washed over a 63 mm sieve, dried in an oven at 60°C, and split into manageable fractions using a brass microsplitter.
Each split sample yielded approximately 300 specimens, which were dry-sieved over a 125 mm sieve, and the foraminifera were picked from the ≥125 mm fraction into cardboard microscope slides.
Selected specimens were photographed using a FEI QUANTA 200 FEG SEM at the Laboratory of Critical Elements.
